verses.sanskrit: श्रीराजोवाचततो महाभागवत उद्धवे निर्गते वनम् ।द्वारवत्यां किमकरोद् भगवान् भूतभावन: ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-rājovācatato mahā-bhāgavatauddhave nirgate vanamdvāravatyāṁ kim akarodbhagavān bhūta-bhāvanaḥ
verses.text: King Parīkṣit said: After the great devotee Uddhava left for the forest, what did the Supreme Personality of Godhead, the protector of all living beings, do in the city of Dvārakā?

verses.sanskrit: ब्रह्मशापोपसंसृष्टे स्वकुले यादवर्षभ: ।प्रेयसीं सर्वनेत्राणां तनुं स कथमत्यजत् ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: brahma-śāpopasaṁsṛṣṭesva-kule yādavarṣabhaḥpreyasīṁ sarva-netrāṇāṁtanuṁ sa katham atyajat
verses.text: After His own dynasty met destruction from the curse of the brāhmaṇas, how could the best of the Yadus give up His body, the dearmost object of all eyes?

verses.sanskrit: प्रत्याक्रष्टुं नयनमबला यत्र लग्नं न शेकु:कर्णाविष्टं न सरति ततो यत् सतामात्मलग्नम् ।यच्छ्रीर्वाचां जनयति रतिं किं नु मानं कवीनांद‍ृष्ट्वा जिष्णोर्युधि रथगतं यच्च तत्साम्यमीयु: ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: pratyākraṣṭuṁ nayanam abalā yatra lagnaṁ na śekuḥkarṇāviṣṭaṁ na sarati tato yat satām ātma-lagnamyac-chrīr vācāṁ janayati ratiṁ kiṁ nu mānaṁ kavīnāṁdṛṣṭvā jiṣṇor yudhi ratha-gataṁ yac ca tat-sāmyam īyuḥ
verses.text: Once their eyes were fixed upon His transcendental form, women were unable to withdraw them, and once that form had entered the ears of the sages and become fixed in their hearts, it would never depart. What to speak of acquiring fame, the great poets who described the beauty of the Lord’s form would have their words invested with transcendentally pleasing attraction. And by seeing that form on Arjuna’s chariot, all the warriors on the battlefield of Kurukṣetra attained the liberation of gaining a spiritual body similar to the Lord’s.

verses.sanskrit: श्री ऋषिरुवाचदिवि भुव्यन्तरिक्षे च महोत्पातान् समुत्थितान् ।द‍ृष्ट्वासीनान् सुधर्मायां कृष्ण: प्राह यदूनिदम् ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī ṛṣir uvācadivi bhuvy antarikṣe camahotpātān samutthitāndṛṣṭvāsīnān su-dharmāyāṁkṛṣṇaḥ prāha yadūn idam
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: Having observed many disturbing signs in the sky, on the earth and in outer space, Lord Kṛṣṇa addressed the Yadus assembled in the Sudharmā council hall as follows.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाचएते घोरा महोत्पाता द्वार्वत्यां यमकेतव: ।मुहूर्तमपि न स्थेयमत्र नो यदुपुङ्गवा: ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvācaete ghorā mahotpātādvārvatyāṁ yama-ketavaḥmuhūrtam api na stheyamatra no yadu-puṅgavāḥ
verses.text: The Supreme Personality of Godhead said: O leaders of the Yadu dynasty, please note all these terrible omens that have appeared in Dvārakā just like the flags of death. We should not remain here a moment longer.

verses.sanskrit: स्‍त्रियो बालाश्च वृद्धाश्च शङ्खोद्धारं व्रजन्त्वित: ।वयं प्रभासं यास्यामो यत्र प्रत्यक् सरस्वती ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: striyo bālāś ca vṛddhāś caśaṅkhoddhāraṁ vrajantv itaḥvayaṁ prabhāsaṁ yāsyāmoyatra pratyak sarasvatī
verses.text: The women, children and old men should leave this city and go to Śaṅkhoddhāra. We shall go to Prabhāsa-kṣetra, where the river Sarasvatī flows toward the west.

verses.sanskrit: तत्राभिषिच्य शुचय उपोष्य सुसमाहिता: ।देवता: पूजयिष्याम: स्‍नपनालेपनार्हणै: ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: tatrābhiṣicya śucayaupoṣya su-samāhitāḥdevatāḥ pūjayiṣyāmaḥsnapanālepanārhaṇaiḥ
verses.text: There we should bathe for purification, fast, and fix our minds in meditation. We should then worship the demigods by bathing their images, anointing them with sandalwood pulp, and presenting them various offerings.

verses.sanskrit: ब्राह्मणांस्तु महाभागान् कृतस्वस्त्ययना वयम् ।गोभूहिरण्यवासोभिर्गजाश्वरथवेश्मभि: ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: brāhmaṇāṁs tu mahā-bhāgānkṛta-svastyayanā vayamgo-bhū-hiraṇya-vāsobhirgajāśva-ratha-veśmabhiḥ
verses.text: After performing the expiatory rituals with the help of greatly fortunate brāhmaṇas, we will worship those brāhmaṇas by offering them cows, land, gold, clothing, elephants, horses, chariots and dwelling places.

verses.sanskrit: विधिरेष ह्यरिष्टघ्नो मङ्गलायनमुत्तमम् ।देवद्विजगवां पूजा भूतेषु परमो भव: ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: vidhir eṣa hy ariṣṭa-ghnomaṅgalāyanam uttamamdeva-dvija-gavāṁ pūjābhūteṣu paramo bhavaḥ
verses.text: This is indeed the appropriate process for counteracting our imminent adversity, and it is sure to bring about the highest good fortune. Such worship of the demigods, brāhmaṇas and cows can earn the highest birth for all living entities.

verses.sanskrit: इति सर्वे समाकर्ण्य यदुवृद्धा मधुद्विष: ।तथेति नौभिरुत्तीर्य प्रभासं प्रययू रथै: ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: iti sarve samākarṇyayadu-vṛddhā madhu-dviṣaḥtatheti naubhir uttīryaprabhāsaṁ prayayū rathaiḥ
verses.text: Having heard these words from Lord Kṛṣṇa, the enemy of Madhu, the elders of the Yadu dynasty gave their assent, saying, “So be it.” After crossing over the ocean in boats, they proceeded on chariots to Prabhāsa.

verses.sanskrit: तस्मिन् भगवतादिष्टं यदुदेवेन यादवा: ।चक्रु: परमया भक्त्या सर्वश्रेयोपबृंहितम् ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasmin bhagavatādiṣṭaṁyadu-devena yādavāḥcakruḥ paramayā bhaktyāsarva-śreyopabṛṁhitam
verses.text: There, with great devotion, the Yādavas performed the religious ceremonies according to the instructions of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, their personal Lord. They also performed various other auspicious rituals.

verses.sanskrit: ततस्तस्मिन् महापानं पपुर्मैरेयकं मधु ।दिष्टविभ्रंशितधियो यद्‌द्रवैर्भ्रश्यते मति: ॥ १२ ॥
verses.transliteration: tatas tasmin mahā-pānaṁpapur maireyakaṁ madhudiṣṭa-vibhraṁśita-dhiyoyad-dravair bhraśyate matiḥ
verses.text: Then, their intelligence covered by Providence, they liberally indulged in drinking the sweet maireya beverage, which can completely intoxicate the mind.

verses.sanskrit: महापानाभिमत्तानां वीराणां द‍ृप्तचेतसाम् ।कृष्णमायाविमूढानां सङ्घर्ष: सुमहानभूत् ॥ १३ ॥
verses.transliteration: mahā-pānābhimattānāṁvīrāṇāṁ dṛpta-cetasāmkṛṣṇa-māyā-vimūḍhānāṁsaṅgharṣaḥ su-mahān abhūt
verses.text: The heroes of the Yadu dynasty became intoxicated from their extravagant drinking and began to feel arrogant. When they were thus bewildered by the personal potency of Lord Kṛṣṇa, a terrible quarrel arose among them.

verses.sanskrit: युयुधु: क्रोधसंरब्धा वेलायामाततायिन: ।धनुर्भिरसिभिर्भल्ल‍ै‌र्गदाभिस्तोमरर्ष्टिभि: ॥ १४ ॥
verses.transliteration: yuyudhuḥ krodha-saṁrabdhāvelāyām ātatāyinaḥdhanurbhir asibhir bhallairgadābhis tomararṣṭibhiḥ
verses.text: Infuriated, they seized their bows and arrows, swords, bhallas, clubs, lances and spears and attacked one another on the shore of the ocean.

verses.sanskrit: पतत्पताकै रथकुञ्जरादिभि:खरोष्ट्रगोभिर्महिषैर्नरैरपि ।मिथ: समेत्याश्वतरै: सुदुर्मदान्यहन्शरैर्दद्भ‍िरिव द्विपा वने ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: patat-patākai ratha-kuñjarādibhiḥkharoṣṭra-gobhir mahiṣair narair apimithaḥ sametyāśvataraiḥ su-durmadānyahan śarair dadbhir iva dvipā vane
verses.text: Riding on elephants and chariots with flags flying, and also on donkeys, camels, bulls, buffalos, mules and even human beings, the extremely enraged warriors came together and violently attacked one another with arrows, just as elephants in the forest attack one another with their tusks.

verses.sanskrit: प्रद्युम्नसाम्बौ युधि रूढमत्सराव्-अक्रूरभोजावनिरुद्धसात्यकी ।सुभद्रसङ्ग्रामजितौ सुदारुणौगदौ सुमित्रासुरथौ समीयतु: ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: pradyumna-sāmbau yudhi rūḍha-matsarāvakrūra-bhojāv aniruddha-sātyakīsubhadra-saṅgrāmajitau su-dāruṇaugadau sumitrā-surathau samīyatuḥ
verses.text: Their mutual enmity aroused, Pradyumna fought fiercely against Sāmba, Akrūra against Kuntibhoja, Aniruddha against Sātyaki, Subhadra against Saṅgrāmajit, Sumitra against Suratha, and the two Gadas against each other.

verses.sanskrit: अन्ये च ये वै निशठोल्मुकादय:सहस्रजिच्छतजिद्भ‍ानुमुख्या: ।अन्योन्यमासाद्य मदान्धकारिताजघ्नुर्मुकुन्देन विमोहिता भृशम् ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: anye ca ye vai niśaṭholmukādayaḥsahasrajic-chatajid-bhānu-mukhyāḥanyonyam āsādya madāndha-kāritājaghnur mukundena vimohitā bhṛśam
verses.text: Others also, such as Niśaṭha, Ulmuka, Sahasrajit, Śatajit and Bhānu, confronted and killed one another, being blinded by intoxication and thus completely bewildered by Lord Mukunda Himself.

verses.sanskrit: दाशार्हवृष्ण्यन्धकभोजसात्वतामध्वर्बुदा माथुरशूरसेना: ।विसर्जना: कुकुरा: कुन्तयश्चमिथस्तु जघ्नु: सुविसृज्य सौहृदम् ॥ १८ ॥
verses.transliteration: dāśārha-vṛṣṇy-andhaka-bhoja-sātvatāmadhv-arbudā māthura-śūrasenāḥvisarjanāḥ kukurāḥ kuntayaś camithas tu jaghnuḥ su-visṛjya sauhṛdam
verses.text: Completely abandoning their natural friendship, the members of the various Yadu clans — the Dāśārhas, Vṛṣṇis and Andhakas, the Bhojas, Sātvatas, Madhus and Arbudas, the Māthuras, Śūrasenas, Visarjanas, Kukuras and Kuntis — all slaughtered one another.

verses.sanskrit: पुत्रा अयुध्यन् पितृभिर्भ्रातृभिश्चस्वस्रीयदौहित्रपितृव्यमातुलै: ।मित्राणि मित्रै: सुहृद: सुहृद्भ‍ि-र्ज्ञातींस्त्वहन् ज्ञातय एव मूढा: ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: putrā ayudhyan pitṛbhir bhrātṛbhiś casvasrīya-dauhitra-pitṛvya-mātulaiḥmitrāṇi mitraiḥ suhṛdaḥ suhṛdbhirjñātīṁs tv ahan jñātaya eva mūḍhāḥ
verses.text: Thus bewildered, sons fought with fathers, brothers with brothers, nephews with paternal and maternal uncles, and grandsons with grandfathers. Friends fought with friends, and well-wishers with well-wishers. In this way intimate friends and relatives all killed one another.

verses.sanskrit: शरेषु हीयमानेषु भज्यमानेसु धन्वसु ।शस्‍त्रेषु क्षीयमानेषु मुष्टिभिर्जह्रुरेरका: ॥ २० ॥
verses.transliteration: śareṣu hīyamāneṣubhajyamāneṣu dhanvasuśastreṣu kṣīyamāneṣumuṣṭibhir jahrur erakāḥ
verses.text: When all their bows had been broken and their arrows and other missiles spent, they seized the tall stalks of cane with their bare hands.

verses.sanskrit: ता वज्रकल्पा ह्यभवन् परिघा मुष्टिना भृता: ।जघ्नुर्द्विषस्तै: कृष्णेन वार्यमाणास्तु तं च ते ॥ २१ ॥
verses.transliteration: tā vajra-kalpā hy abhavanparighā muṣṭinā bhṛtāḥjaghnur dviṣas taiḥ kṛṣṇenavāryamāṇās tu taṁ ca te
verses.text: As soon as they took these cane stalks in their fists, the stalks changed into iron rods as hard as thunderbolts. With these weapons the warriors began attacking one another again and again, and when Lord Kṛṣṇa tried to stop them they attacked Him as well.

verses.sanskrit: प्रत्यनीकं मन्यमाना बलभद्रं च मोहिता: ।हन्तुं कृतधियो राजन्नापन्ना आततायिन: ॥ २२ ॥
verses.transliteration: pratyanīkaṁ manyamānābalabhadraṁ ca mohitāḥhantuṁ kṛta-dhiyo rājannāpannā ātatāyinaḥ
verses.text: In their confused state, O King, they also mistook Lord Balarāma for an enemy. Weapons in hand, they ran toward Him with the intention of killing Him.

verses.sanskrit: अथ तावपि सङ्‌क्रु‌द्धावुद्यम्य कुरुनन्दन ।एरकामुष्टिपरिघौ चरन्तौ जघ्नतुर्युधि ॥ २३ ॥
verses.transliteration: atha tāv api saṅkruddhāvudyamya kuru-nandanaerakā-muṣṭi-parighaucarantau jaghnatur yudhi
verses.text: O son of the Kurus, Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma then became very angry. Picking up cane stalks, They moved about within the battle and began to kill with these clubs.

verses.sanskrit: ब्रह्मशापोपसृष्टानां कृष्णमायावृतात्मनाम् ।स्पर्धाक्रोध: क्षयं निन्ये वैणवोऽग्निर्यथा वनम् ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: brahma-śāpopasṛṣṭānāṁkṛṣṇa-māyāvṛtātmanāmspardhā-krodhaḥ kṣayaṁ ninyevaiṇavo ’gnir yathā vanam
verses.text: The violent anger of these warriors, who were overcome by the brāhmaṇas’ curse and bewildered by Lord Kṛṣṇa’s illusory potency, now led them to their annihilation, just as a fire that starts in a bamboo grove destroys the entire forest.

verses.sanskrit: एवं नष्टेषु सर्वेषु कुलेषु स्वेषु केशव: ।अवतारितो भुवो भार इति मेनेऽवशेषित: ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: evaṁ naṣṭeṣu sarveṣukuleṣu sveṣu keśavaḥavatārito bhuvo bhāraiti mene ’vaśeṣitaḥ
verses.text: When all the members of His own dynasty were thus destroyed, Lord Kṛṣṇa thought to Himself that at last the burden of the earth had been removed.

verses.sanskrit: राम: समुद्रवेलायां योगमास्थाय पौरुषम् ।तत्याज लोकं मानुष्यं संयोज्यात्मानमात्मनि ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: rāmaḥ samudra-velāyāṁyogam āsthāya pauruṣamtatyāja lokaṁ mānuṣyaṁsaṁyojyātmānam ātmani
verses.text: Lord Balarāma then sat down on the shore of the ocean and fixed Himself in meditation upon the Supreme Personality of Godhead. Merging Himself within Himself, He gave up this mortal world.

verses.sanskrit: रामनिर्याणमालोक्य भगवान् देवकीसुत: ।निषसाद धरोपस्थे तुष्णीमासाद्य पिप्पलम् ॥ २७ ॥
verses.transliteration: rāma-niryāṇam ālokyabhagavān devakī-sutaḥniṣasāda dharopasthetuṣṇīm āsādya pippalam
verses.text: Lord Kṛṣṇa, the son of Devakī, having seen the departure of Lord Rāma, sat down silently on the ground under a nearby pippala tree.

verses.sanskrit: बिभ्रच्चतुर्भुजं रूपं भ्राजिष्णु प्रभया स्वया ।दिशो वितिमिरा: कुर्वन् विधूम इव पावक: ॥ २८ ॥श्रीवत्साङ्कं घनश्यामं तप्तहाटकवर्चसम् ।कौशेयाम्बरयुग्मेन परिवीतं सुमङ्गलम् ॥ २९ ॥सुन्दरस्मितवक्त्राब्जं नीलकुन्तलमण्डितम् ।पुण्डरीकाभिरामाक्षं स्फुरन्मकरकुण्डलम् ॥ ३० ॥कटिसूत्रब्रह्मसूत्रकिरीटकटकाङ्गदै: ।हारनूपुरमुद्राभि: कौस्तुभेन विराजितम् ॥ ३१ ॥वनमालापरीताङ्गं मूर्तिमद्भ‍िर्निजायुधै: ।कृत्वोरौ दक्षिणे पादमासीनं पङ्कजारुणम् ॥ ३२ ॥
verses.transliteration: bibhrac catur-bhujaṁ rūpaṁbhrājiṣṇu prabhayā svayādiśo vitimirāḥ kurvanvidhūma iva pāvakaḥ
verses.text: The Lord was exhibiting His brilliantly effulgent four-armed form, the radiance of which, just like a smokeless fire, dissipated the darkness in all directions. His complexion was the color of a dark blue cloud and His effulgence the color of molten gold, and His all-auspicious form bore the mark of Śrīvatsa. A beautiful smile graced His lotus face, locks of dark blue hair adorned His head, His lotus eyes were very attractive, and His shark-shaped earrings glittered. He wore a pair of silken garments, an ornamental belt, the sacred thread, bracelets and arm ornaments, along with a helmet, the Kaustubha jewel, necklaces, anklets and other royal emblems. Encircling His body were flower garlands and His personal weapons in their embodied forms. As He sat He held His left foot, with its lotus-red sole, upon His right thigh.

verses.sanskrit: मुषलावशेषाय:खण्डकृतेषुर्लुब्धको जरा ।मृगास्याकारं तच्चरणं विव्याध मृगशङ्कया ॥ ३३ ॥
verses.transliteration: muṣalāvaśeṣāyaḥ-khaṇḍa-kṛteṣur lubdhako jarāmṛgāsyākāraṁ tac-caraṇaṁvivyādha mṛga-śaṅkayā
verses.text: Just then a hunter named Jarā, who had approached the place, mistook the Lord’s foot for a deer’s face. Thinking he had found his prey, Jarā pierced the foot with his arrow, which he had fashioned from the remaining iron fragment of Sāmba’s club.

verses.sanskrit: चतुर्भुजं तं पुरुषं द‍ृष्ट्वा स कृतकिल्बिष: ।भीत: पपात शिरसा पादयोरसुरद्विष: ॥ ३४ ॥
verses.transliteration: catur-bhujaṁ taṁ puruṣaṁdṛṣṭvā sa kṛta-kilbiṣaḥbhītaḥ papāta śirasāpādayor asura-dviṣaḥ
verses.text: Then, seeing that four-armed personality, the hunter became terrified of the offense he had committed, and he fell down, placing his head upon the feet of the enemy of the demons.

verses.sanskrit: अजानता कृतमिदं पापेन मधुसूदन ।क्षन्तुमर्हसि पापस्य उत्तम:श्लोक मेऽनघ ॥ ३५ ॥
verses.transliteration: ajānatā kṛtam idaṁpāpena madhusūdanakṣantum arhasi pāpasyauttamaḥśloka me ’nagha
verses.text: Jarā said: O Lord Madhusūdana, I am a most sinful person. I have committed this act out of ignorance. O purest Lord, O Uttamaḥśloka, please forgive this sinner.

verses.sanskrit: यस्यानुस्मरणं नृणामज्ञानध्वान्तनाशनम् ।वदन्ति तस्य ते विष्णो मयासाधु कृतं प्रभो ॥ ३६ ॥
verses.transliteration: yasyānusmaraṇaṁ nṛṇāmajñāna-dhvānta-nāśanamvadanti tasya te viṣṇomayāsādhu kṛtaṁ prabho
verses.text: O Lord Viṣṇu, the learned say that for any man, constant remembrance of You will destroy the darkness of ignorance. O master, I have wronged You!

verses.sanskrit: तन्माशु जहि वैकुण्ठ पाप्मानं मृगलुब्धकम् ।यथा पुनरहं त्वेवं न कुर्यां सदतिक्रमम् ॥ ३७ ॥
verses.transliteration: tan māśu jahi vaikuṇṭhapāpmānaṁ mṛga-lubdhakamyathā punar ahaṁ tv evaṁna kuryāṁ sad-atikramam
verses.text: Therefore, O Lord of Vaikuṇṭha, please kill this sinful hunter of animals immediately so he may not again commit such offenses against saintly persons.

verses.sanskrit: यस्यात्मयोगरचितं न विदुर्विरिञ्चोरुद्रादयोऽस्य तनया: पतयो गिरां ये ।त्वन्मायया पिहितद‍ृष्टय एतदञ्ज:किं तस्य ते वयमसद्गतयो गृणीम: ॥ ३८ ॥
verses.transliteration: yasyātma-yoga-racitaṁ na vidur viriñcorudrādayo ’sya tanayāḥ patayo girāṁ yetvan-māyayā pihita-dṛṣṭaya etad añjaḥkiṁ tasya te vayam asad-gatayo gṛṇīmaḥ
verses.text: Neither Brahmā nor his sons, headed by Rudra, nor any of the great sages who are masters of the Vedic mantras can understand the function of Your mystic power. Because Your illusory potency has covered their sight, they remain ignorant of how Your mystic power works. Therefore, what can I, such a low-born person, possibly say?

verses.sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाचमा भैर्जरे त्वमुत्तिष्ठ काम एष कृतो हि मे ।याहि त्वं मदनुज्ञात: स्वर्गं सुकृतिनां पदम् ॥ ३९ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvācamā bhair jare tvam uttiṣṭhakāma eṣa kṛto hi meyāhi tvaṁ mad-anujñātaḥsvargaṁ su-kṛtināṁ padam
verses.text: The Supreme Personality of Godhead said: My dear Jarā, do not fear. Please get up. What has been done is actually My own desire. With My permission, go now to the abode of the pious, the spiritual world.

verses.sanskrit: इत्यादिष्टो भगवता कृष्णेनेच्छाशरीरिणा ।त्रि: परिक्रम्य तं नत्वा विमानेन दिवं ययौ ॥ ४० ॥
verses.transliteration: ity ādiṣṭo bhagavatākṛṣṇenecchā-śarīriṇātriḥ parikramya taṁ natvāvimānena divaṁ yayau
verses.text: So instructed by the Supreme Lord Kṛṣṇa, who assumes His transcendental body by His own will, the hunter circumambulated the Lord three times and bowed down to Him. Then the hunter departed in an airplane that had appeared just to carry him to the spiritual sky.

verses.sanskrit: दारुक: कृष्णपदवीमन्विच्छन्नधिगम्य ताम् ।वायुं तुलसिकामोदमाघ्रायाभिमुखं ययौ ॥ ४१ ॥
verses.transliteration: dārukaḥ kṛṣṇa-padavīmanvicchann adhigamya tāmvāyuṁ tulasikāmodamāghrāyābhimukhaṁ yayau
verses.text: At that time Dāruka was searching for his master, Kṛṣṇa. As he neared the place where the Lord was sitting, he perceived the aroma of tulasī flowers in the breeze and went in its direction.

verses.sanskrit: तं तत्र तिग्मद्युभिरायुधैर्वृतंह्यश्वत्थमूले कृतकेतनं पतिम् ।स्‍नेहप्लुतात्मा निपपात पादयोरथादवप्लुत्य सबाष्पलोचन: ॥ ४२ ॥
verses.transliteration: taṁ tatra tigma-dyubhir āyudhair vṛtaṁhy aśvattha-mūle kṛta-ketanaṁ patimsneha-plutātmā nipapāta pādayorathād avaplutya sa-bāṣpa-locanaḥ
verses.text: Upon seeing Lord Kṛṣṇa resting at the foot of a banyan tree, surrounded by His shining weapons, Dāruka could not control the affection he felt in his heart. His eyes filled with tears as he rushed down from the chariot and fell at the Lord’s feet.

verses.sanskrit: अपश्यतस्त्वच्चरणाम्बुजं प्रभोद‍ृष्टि: प्रणष्टा तमसि प्रविष्टा ।दिशो न जाने न लभे च शान्तिंयथा निशायामुडुपे प्रणष्टे ॥ ४३ ॥
verses.transliteration: apaśyatas tvac-caraṇāmbujaṁ prabhodṛṣṭiḥ praṇaṣṭā tamasi praviṣṭādiśo na jāne na labhe ca śāntiṁyathā niśāyām uḍupe praṇaṣṭe
verses.text: Dāruka said: Just as on a moonless night people are merged into darkness and cannot find their way, now that I have lost sight of Your lotus feet, my Lord, I have lost my vision and am wandering blindly in darkness. I cannot tell my direction, nor can I find any peace.

verses.sanskrit: इति ब्रुवति सूते वै रथो गरुडलाञ्छन: ।खमुत्पपात राजेन्द्र साश्वध्वज उदीक्षत: ॥ ४४ ॥
verses.transliteration: iti bruvati sūte vairatho garuḍa-lāñchanaḥkham utpapāta rājendrasāśva-dhvaja udīkṣataḥ
verses.text: [Śukadeva Gosvāmī continued:] O foremost of kings, while the chariot driver was still speaking, before his very eyes the Lord’s chariot rose up into the sky along with its horses and its flag, which was marked with the emblem of Garuḍa.

verses.sanskrit: तमन्वगच्छन् दिव्यानि विष्णुप्रहरणानि च ।तेनातिविस्मितात्मानं सूतमाह जनार्दन: ॥ ४५ ॥
verses.transliteration: tam anvagacchan divyāniviṣṇu-praharaṇāni catenāti-vismitātmānaṁsūtam āha janārdanaḥ
verses.text: All the divine weapons of Viṣṇu rose up and followed the chariot. The Lord, Janārdana, then spoke to His chariot driver, who was most astonished to see all this.

verses.sanskrit: गच्छ द्वारवतीं सूत ज्ञातीनां निधनं मिथ: ।सङ्कर्षणस्य निर्याणं बन्धुभ्यो ब्रूहि मद्दशाम् ॥ ४६ ॥
verses.transliteration: gaccha dvāravatīṁ sūtajñātīnāṁ nidhanaṁ mithaḥsaṅkarṣaṇasya niryāṇaṁbandhubhyo brūhi mad-daśām
verses.text: O driver, go to Dvārakā and tell Our family members how their loved ones destroyed one another. Also tell them of the disappearance of Lord Saṅkarṣaṇa and of My present condition.

verses.sanskrit: द्वारकायां च न स्थेयं भवद्भ‍िश्च स्वबन्धुभि: ।मया त्यक्तां यदुपुरीं समुद्र: प्लावयिष्यति ॥ ४७ ॥
verses.transliteration: dvārakāyāṁ ca na stheyaṁbhavadbhiś ca sva-bandhubhiḥmayā tyaktāṁ yadu-purīṁsamudraḥ plāvayiṣyati
verses.text: You and your relatives should not remain in Dvārakā, the capital of the Yadus, because once I have abandoned that city it will be inundated by the ocean.

verses.sanskrit: स्वं स्वं परिग्रहं सर्वे आदाय पितरौ च न: ।अर्जुनेनाविता: सर्व इन्द्रप्रस्थं गमिष्यथ ॥ ४८ ॥
verses.transliteration: svaṁ svaṁ parigrahaṁ sarveādāya pitarau ca naḥarjunenāvitāḥ sarvaindraprasthaṁ gamiṣyatha
verses.text: You should all take your own families, together with My parents, and under Arjuna’s protection go to Indraprastha.

verses.sanskrit: त्वं तु मद्धर्ममास्थाय ज्ञाननिष्ठ उपेक्षक: ।मन्मायारचितामेतां विज्ञायोपशमं व्रज ॥ ४९ ॥
verses.transliteration: tvaṁ tu mad-dharmam āsthāyajñāna-niṣṭha upekṣakaḥman-māyā-racitām etāṁvijñayopaśamaṁ vraja
verses.text: You, Dāruka, should be firmly situated in devotion to Me, remaining fixed in spiritual knowledge and unattached to material considerations. Understanding these pastimes to be a display of My illusory potency, you should remain peaceful.

verses.sanskrit: इत्युक्तस्तं परिक्रम्य नमस्कृत्य पुन: पुन: ।तत्पादौ शीर्ष्ण्युपाधाय दुर्मना: प्रययौ पुरीम् ॥ ५० ॥
verses.transliteration: ity uktas taṁ parikramyanamaskṛtya punaḥ punaḥtat-pādau śīrṣṇy upādhāyadurmanāḥ prayayau purīm
verses.text: Thus ordered, Dāruka circumambulated the Lord and offered obeisances to Him again and again. He placed Lord Kṛṣṇa’s lotus feet upon his head and then with a sad heart went back to the city.
